mission.title: argo: feat:  Extra Helm values from external git repo #5826
mission.description:
# Feature: External Helm values from git

This PR allows for external values.yaml from other git repos in a helm installation. 

**Sample application.yaml**

```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: external-test
  finalizers:
    - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
    targetRevision: 8.5.8
    helm:
      valueFiles:
        - values.yaml
      externalValueFiles:

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description:
# Feature: External Helm values from git

This PR allows for external values.yaml from other git repos in a helm installation. 

**Sample application.yaml**

```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
k

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: external-test
  finalizers:
    - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
    targetRevision: 8.5.8
    helm:
      valueFiles:
        - values.yaml
      externalValueFiles:
        - repoURL: https://github.com/KaiReichart/argo-test-values.git
          targetRevision: main
          valueFiles:
            - values.yaml
    
```

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
> @rouke-broersma this is exactly what the field `targetRevision` is for. You could have tags (or branches) in your repos that contain the version specific value files, and then reference these in your `application.yaml`.
> So you can either reference `main` in `application.yaml` and manually keep your 2 repos in sync or reference a specific version branch and update `application.yaml` when you want to update the application.
> 
> However this isn't a problem specific to this implementation, but rather to the whole approach of splitting chart and values into different repos.

We are planning to put the application.yaml and the values.yaml in the same repo. Argo could theoretically (perhaps argo already does this?) tag the application with the git revision it comes from (app-of-apps pattern). If I could then specify in the application something akin to

```
externalValueFiles:
- fromApplicationRepo: true
  withApplicationRepoRevision: true
  valueFiles:
   - values.yaml
```

That would be epic and solve the problem of keeping them in sync (if you use the same repo for the application and values).

I understand of course that this is not what you're contributing in the PR but do you think it would be a feasible solution to the sync issue?

          targetRevision: main
          valueFiles:
            - values.yaml
    chart: mysql
  destination:
    server: 'https://kubernetes.default.svc'
    namespace: default

That would be epic and solve the problem of keeping them in sync (if you use the same repo for the application and values).

I understand of course that this is not what you're contributing in the PR but do you think it would be a feasible solution to the sync issue?
@KaiReichart Did you want me to send you another PR to fix the codegen conflict since I caused it? We really need to coordinate this w/ approval and merge, or else it's going to keep cropping up as other PRs get merged. 
@tinkerborg sure please do, this is only going to be a case of running `make codegen` to generate the necessary files.  
> @tinkerborg sure please do, this is only going to be a case of running `make codegen` to generate the necessary files.

Hmm, actually your repo needs to be updated w/ upstream master. I tried fetching your branch, merging argoproj/master and running the codegen. The conflict resolved fine but the commit history on the PR was a mess. Might be easier for you to do it. This is going to need to be repeated every time an upstream change involves codegen, until this branch is merged though...
I'll do it, but this is the 3rd time I'm doing this and none of the ArgoCD maintainers has even acknowledged this PR, so I'm a bit hesitant about always keeping it up to date with the generated code until the maintainers at least signal any kind of interest in this feature...
